title: Escarole Bean Soup
cScaleSummary: C0 because the soup is built almost entirely from cholesterol-free ingredients including navy beans, brown jasmine rice, escarole, aromatics, olive oil, pesto, lemon, and seasonings. The chicken stock is present in a modest enough amount that the per-serving cholesterol remains effectively negligible.
ldlActiveSummary: LDL-Active because the soup is built around navy beans, greens, aromatics, and a modest amount of rice, with flavor developed through broth, herbs, pesto, and lemon rather than saturated fat. It is legume-forward, vegetable-rich, and supportive of LDL lowering.
intro: This soup borrows from two Italian instincts: the Ligurian habit of stirring pesto into soup at the end, and the broader tradition of building a meal from beans, greens, broth, and a little starch. Navy beans bring creaminess, brown jasmine rice gives the bowl body, and escarole adds a gentle bitterness that keeps the pesto bright. The result is rustic, practical, and fresh, with the comfort of a pantry soup and a vivid herbaceous lift.

items.prep: washed and chopped into 1- to 2-inch strips

stepGroups.title: Cook the Beans
items: Soak the navy beans overnight, then drain. Cook them in fresh water until tender. In a pressure cooker, cook at high pressure for 20 minutes and allow a natural release. On the stovetop, simmer for 45 minutes, or until fully tender. Drain and set aside.

stepGroups.title: Cook the Rice
items: Combine the brown jasmine rice, water, and pinch of salt in a small pot. Bring to a boil, cover, reduce to a low simmer, and cook until nearly tender, about 30 minutes. Let stand covered for 5 minutes, then fluff and set aside.

stepGroups.title: Build the Soup Base
items: Heat the olive oil in a soup pot over medium heat. Add the onion, carrot, celery, and garlic and cook for about 5 minutes, until softened and fragrant.

stepGroups.title: Simmer the Soup
items: Add the cooked beans, chicken stock, bay leaf, and thyme. Bring to a simmer and cook for 10 minutes so the flavors meld.

stepGroups.title: Add the Greens and Rice
items: Stir in the escarole and cooked rice. Simmer for 5 to 7 minutes, until the escarole is softened but still lively and the rice is fully tender.

stepGroups.title: Finish the Soup
items: Remove the bay leaf. Stir in the pesto and lemon juice. Season with salt and pepper.
